Comments: Whole-genome analysis of multidrug-resistant Pseudomonas aeruginosa isolated from Bangladesh

Pseudomonas aeruginosa causes nosocomial infection and can be associated with deadly infections. It is specifically life-threatening to burn patients. This bacterium is intrinsically resistant to different antibiotics used for treatment purposes. However, the acquired resistance to antibiotics like carbapenem has reduced the opportunity to treat severe infections using this antibiotic.
Pseudomonas aeruginosa DMC-27b is a multidrug resistant clinical isolate collected from urine of an ICU patient with UTI.
